Title: Create user profile
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/auth/profile with valid Bearer token
2. Include displayName in request body
Expected Results: 201 Created; response body contains the new profile with displayName

Title: Create profile — displayName missing
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/auth/profile with valid token
2. Send empty request body

Title: Create profile — displayName whitespace only
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/auth/profile with valid token
2. Send displayName as spaces only

Title: Create profile — already exists
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/auth/profile with valid token
2. Profile already exists for this user

Title: Create profile — no auth token
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/auth/profile
2. Omit Authorization header

Title: Get own profile
Steps: 1. Send GET /api/auth/me with valid Bearer token

Title: Get own profile — profile not found
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/auth/me with valid token
2. No profile exists for this user in DB

Title: Get own profile — no auth token
Steps: 1. Send GET /api/auth/me without Authorization header

Title: Update profile
Steps:
1. Send PATCH /api/auth/me with valid token
2. Include displayName and bio in body

Title: Update profile — displayName missing
Steps:
1. Send PATCH /api/auth/me with valid token
2. Omit displayName from body

Title: Update profile — empty bio clears bio
Steps:
1. Send PATCH /api/auth/me with valid token
2. Send displayName and bio as empty string
Expected Results: 200 OK; bio stored as null in DB
Actual Results: 200 returned; bio null in DB call

Title: Update profile — upload avatar image
Steps:
1. Send PATCH /api/auth/me with valid token
2. Attach an image file as multipart/form-data with displayName
Expected Results: 200 OK; uploadBuffer called with avatars folder; avatarUrl stored
Actual Results: 200 returned; uploadBuffer called with correct folder

Title: Update profile — no auth token
Steps: 1. Send PATCH /api/auth/me without Authorization header

Title: Get public video (unauthenticated)
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/videos/:videoId without auth
2. Video exists and is public
Expected Results: 200 OK; video data with averageRating returned; userRating is undefined

Title: Get video — invalid UUID format
Steps: 1. Send GET /api/videos/not-a-valid-uuid

Title: Get video — video does not exist
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/videos/:videoId with valid UUID
2. No video found in DB

Title: Get private video — unauthenticated
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/videos/:videoId without auth
2. Video exists but isPublic is false

Title: Get private video — viewer is creator
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/videos/:videoId with creator token
2. Video is private but belongs to this user
Expected Results: 200 OK; creator can view own private video

Title: Get video — includes viewer's rating
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/videos/:videoId with token
2. Viewer has previously rated the video
Expected Results: 200 OK; userRating field included with viewer's score

Title: Get video comments — list returned
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/videos/:videoId/comments
2. Video is public; comments exist
Expected Results: 200 OK; array of comments with user info ordered by creation date

Title: Get video comments — no comments
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/videos/:videoId/comments
2. Video exists but has no comments

Title: Get video comments — video not found
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/videos/:videoId/comments
2. Video does not exist in DB

Title: Post comment on video
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/comments with token
2. Include non-empty content in body
Expected Results: 201 Created; comment with user info returned

Title: Post comment — empty content
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/comments with token
2. Send content as whitespace-only string

Title: Post comment — content field missing
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/comments with token
2. Omit content from request body

Title: Post comment — no auth token
Steps: 1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/comments without token

Title: Rate a video
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/ratings with token
2. Include integer rating 1–5 in body
Expected Results: 200 OK; averageRating and userRating in response

Title: Rate video — rating below minimum (0)
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/ratings with token
2. Send rating: 0

Title: Rate video — rating above maximum (6)
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/ratings with token
2. Send rating: 6

Title: Rate video — non-integer rating
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/ratings with token
2. Send rating: 3.5

Title: Rate video — no auth token
Steps: 1. Send POST /api/videos/:videoId/ratings without token

Title: Upload video — no video file
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos with token
2. Include title and genre but attach no video file
Expected Results: 400 Bad Request; "Video file is required" message

Title: Upload video — title missing
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos with token
2. Attach video file but omit title field

Title: Upload video — invalid genre
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos with token
2. Attach video file and title but use unknown genre
Expected Results: 400 Bad Request; "genre must be one of" message

Title: Upload video — success
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos with token
2. Attach video file; provide title and valid genre
Expected Results: 201 Created; video record returned with title and genre

Title: Upload video with thumbnail
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos with token
2. Attach both video and thumbnail files with title and genre

Title: Upload video — no auth token
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/videos without token
2. Include all required fields

Title: Browse channels — unauthenticated
Steps: 1. Send GET /api/users without auth
Expected Results: 200 OK; array of channels with subscriberCount; isSubscribed false for all

Title: Browse channels — isSubscribed reflects viewer
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/users with token
2. Viewer is subscribed to one channel
Expected Results: 200 OK; isSubscribed true for followed channel
Actual Results: 200 returned; isSubscribed: true on followed channel

Title: Browse channels — empty platform
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/users
2. No user profiles exist in DB

Title: Get user profile — invalid UUID
Steps: 1. Send GET /api/users/not-a-valid-uuid

Title: Get user profile — user not found
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/users/:userId with valid UUID
2. No user exists in DB

Title: Get user profile — public view
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/users/:userId without auth
2. User exists and has public videos
Expected Results: 200 OK; profile with counts; isOwnProfile false; isSubscribed false; public videos

Title: Get user profile — isOwnProfile flag
Steps: 1. Send GET /api/users/:userId with own userId and token

Title: Get user profile — isSubscribed flag
Steps:
1. Send GET /api/users/:userId with token
2. Viewer is subscribed to this channel

Title: Subscribe to a channel
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/users/:userId/subscribe with token
2. Target user exists; viewer not yet subscribed
Expected Results: 200 OK; isSubscribed true; updated subscriberCount; subscription created in DB

Title: Subscribe — already subscribed (idempotent)
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/users/:userId/subscribe with token
2. Viewer is already subscribed
Expected Results: 200 OK; isSubscribed true; no duplicate subscription created

Title: Subscribe — self subscription
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/users/:userId/subscribe with token
2. userId in URL matches authenticated user
Expected Results: 400 Bad Request; "Cannot subscribe to yourself" message

Title: Subscribe — channel not found
Steps:
1. Send POST /api/users/:userId/subscribe with token
2. Target userId does not exist

Title: Subscribe — no auth token
Steps: 1. Send POST /api/users/:userId/subscribe without token

Title: Unsubscribe from a channel
Steps:
1. Send DELETE /api/users/:userId/subscribe with token
2. Viewer is currently subscribed

Title: Unsubscribe — not subscribed (no-op)
Steps:
1. Send DELETE /api/users/:userId/subscribe with token
2. No existing subscription in DB
Expected Results: 200 OK; isSubscribed false; no error thrown

Title: Unsubscribe — invalid UUID
Steps: 1. Send DELETE /api/users/bad-uuid/subscribe with token

Title: Unsubscribe — no auth token
Steps: 1. Send DELETE /api/users/:userId/subscribe without token
